minutes_briefs.title: Sections of BC Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act take effect April 7, 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: On April 7, 2026, the BC government brought sections 5 to 8 of the Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act, 2025 into force. This means certain legal changes that were passed in 2025 are now active law. The specific changes affect how some legal disputes and contracts are handled, but the regulation itself does not list the exact new rules — it only turns on the sections of the earlier act. Business owners should check the original act (S.B.C. 2025, c. 16) to see what rules now apply to them.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act, 2025 (S.B.C. 2025, c. 16) to identify which new rules affect your business.

key_dates.label: BC Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act sections 5–8 take effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 48/2026
Made under: Attorney General Statutes Amendment Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: April 7, 2026

B.C. Reg. 48/2026, deposited April 7, 2026, under theATTORNEY GENERAL STATUTES AMENDMENT ACT, 2025[section 23]. Order in Council 120/2026, approved and ordered April 7, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that sections 5 to 8 of theAttorney General Statutes Amendment Act, 2025, S.B.C. 2025, c. 16, are brought into force. — N. SHARMA,Attorney General and Deputy Premier; R. PARMAR,Presiding Member of the Executive Council.
